title: Intricately Fried Australian Chinese Chinese Broccoli Spring Rolls
description: Savory and springy, these crowd-pleasing Australian Chinese spring rolls feature Chinese broccoli and are fried to a crispy perfection, served in a banana leaf vessel, making them a jain-friendly delight.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 180 degrees Celsius.
In a large bowl, mix together the chopped Chinese broccoli, minced garlic, and grated ginger.
Lay a spring roll wrapper on a flat surface and place a tablespoon of the broccoli mixture in the center.
Brush the edges of the wrapper with sesame oil and fold the wrapper into a triangle, sealing the filling inside.
Repeat the process with the remaining wrappers and filling.
When the oil is hot, carefully place a few of the spring rolls into the pan and fry until golden brown.
Remove the spring rolls from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the spring rolls hot in a banana leaf vessel, garnished with a sprinkle of salt.
